title: The RealReal Announces First Quarter 2026 Results
text: The RealReal reported strong Q1 2026 results with gross merchandise value (GMV) reaching $606 million, a 24% increase year-over-year. Total revenue grew 19% to $190 million, while adjusted EBITDA improved significantly to $13.1 million (6.9% margin) from $4.1 million (2.6% margin) in the prior year period. CEO Rati Levesque highlighted the company's momentum, stating GMV was up 24% and adjusted EBITDA margin expanded over 400 basis points. Net income was $39 million (20.5% of revenue), down from $62 million in Q1 2025. Gross profit increased $21 million year-over-year to $141 million. Active buyers reached 1.083 million (trailing twelve months), up 10% from prior year. Average order value increased 15% to $646 versus Q1 2025. The company raised its annual outlook: GMV of $2.42-$2.47 billion, total revenue of $770-$784 million, and adjusted EBITDA of $59-$67 million. Q2 2026 projections estimate GMV of $590-$600 million and total revenue of $186-$189 million.
